notes: Country-level VIZA RAG seed for south_africa. This file is the source of truth for this country's visitor/tourism visa knowledge and is designed to evolve alongside the country-specific form-filling workflow.

documents.title: South Africa Visitor Visa for Tourism or Business

chunks.title: Chinese passport tourism route for South Africa
chunks.content: For Chinese ordinary passport holders planning short tourism travel to South Africa, VIZA AI should consider the South Africa visitor visa or eVisa route for tourism/business visitor cases. First confirm nationality, residence, itinerary, stay length, funds, return ticket, accommodation, children travelling, and yellow-fever origin or transit. Do not treat Chinese ordinary passport tourism as automatically visa-free. Keep advice limited to tourism/visitor travel unless the user states work, study, residence, immigration, journalism, volunteering or another regulated purpose.

chunks.title: South Africa visitor visa scope
chunks.content: South African Government guidance says visitors who need a visa must obtain a valid visa before entering South Africa. A visitor visa is issued for tourism or business purposes and is valid for a period of 90 days. VIZA AI should not recommend this route for work, study, long-term residence, volunteering or paid activities without further checks.

chunks.title: South Africa document direction
chunks.content: For South Africa tourist visa questions, ask for passport nationality, residence country, itinerary, funds, return/onward ticket, accommodation, employment/study ties, and whether children are travelling. Also ask about yellow-fever transit/origin and current eVisa availability for the applicant nationality and residence country.

documents.title: South Africa Application Form and Document Intake Requirements

chunks.title: South Africa application channel and form scope
chunks.content: South Africa visitor visa/eVisa route Official source URLs reviewed for this seed: https://www.gov.za/services/temporary-residence/visa. This RAG chunk is for visitor/tourism-oriented intake and form-filling preparation; route work, study, residence, immigration, journalism or other regulated purposes to a separate country workflow.

chunks.title: South Africa fields to collect before filling the form
chunks.content: identity, passport, nationality/residence, travel purpose, itinerary, accommodation, funds, employment/study, family/minor details and yellow-fever origin/transit

chunks.title: South Africa supporting documents and review checklist
chunks.content: passport, application form, photo, proof of funds, return/onward ticket, accommodation/invitation, employment/study evidence, minor documents and yellow-fever certificate where required Workflow guardrails: VIZA should separate eligibility check, form filling, document upload, payment and post-approval travel/entry checks. Do not guarantee entry; border authorities make the final decision.

chunks.title: Standard passport identity-field answer rules
chunks.content: Standard-answer identity fields must be answered from the applicant's passport biodata page, MRZ, official identity document, or the official dropdown options. This applies to passport number, name, date of birth, sex, nationality, issuing country, issue date, expiry date, issuing authority, place of issue, and passport/document type. For issuing authority or place-of-issue questions, do not infer the answer from the pickup city, residence city, application country, or travel destination; copy the exact Authority / Issuing authority / Place of issue wording printed on the passport if present. For Chinese ordinary passports, newer valid passports may show National Immigration Administration, PRC / 中华人民共和国国家移民管理局; older valid passports may show MPS Exit & Entry Administration / 公安部出入境管理局. If the passport prints a different authority, use the passport text. For passport type, select Ordinary / Regular / Normal only for ordinary personal passports; select Diplomatic, Official, Service, Special, Travel document, Refugee, or Other only when the document explicitly indicates that type. Official sources reviewed for this standard field rule include China National Immigration Administration certificate-issuing-authority notices and the Passport Law of the People's Republic of China; the passport biodata page remains the final source for the applicant's individual answer.
